attacking_style: Breakaway specialist who attacks early (60km+ out) to establish position, then leverages tactical positioning and cornering in final 100-200m rather than pure speed. Prioritizes jersey competitions over stage wins when in breakaways.
team_tactics: Team executes repeated mid-race attacks to set him up, but struggles with final positioning and sprint train coordination. Often tactically isolated in decisive moments despite aggressive teammate support earlier.
terrain_preference: Excels on uphill finishes and wet/technical courses where positioning trumps pure watts. Thrives in breakaway scenarios on rolling terrain. Exposed on sustained steep gradients and when pure sprinting speed required.
vulnerability: Poor timing judgment causes premature neutralization of bridging moves. Burns matches defending intermediate objectives (KOM points), leaving nothing for finale. Struggles when team positioning fails in final kilometers.
key_patterns: Breakaway specialist: multiple wins from 60km+ breaks, thrives in numerical stalemate situations (4 observations)
Corner/positioning tactics: uses technical sections in final 100-200m to overcome speed deficit (3 observations)
KOM jersey obsession: attacks breakaway companions early for points, weakening stage-winning cooperation (3 observations)
Bridging diesel: rare ability to bridge gaps solo then still compete in finish (3 observations)
Timing misjudgment: premature moves immediately neutralized, goes too early on uphill finishes (3 observations)
Team coordination failures: domestiques work hard but positioning/sprint train execution poor in crunch (2 observations)
form_trajectory: negative: 2022 Vuelta showed team coordination breakdown despite earlier breakaway success pattern. 2021 featured multiple tactical mistimings and missed selections.
built_from: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 2
